label: Giza Pyramid Complex
sub: Great Pyramid of Khufu (2560 BCE), Khafre, Menkaure & the Great Sphinx. UNESCO World Heritage Site.

label: Saqqara Necropolis
sub: Step Pyramid of Djoser (2650 BCE) — world's first monumental stone structure, built by architect Imhotep.

label: Karnak Temple Complex
sub: Largest ancient religious site in the world, built continuously from Middle Kingdom through Ptolemaic era. Dedicated to Amun-Ra.

label: Valley of the Kings
sub: New Kingdom royal necropolis — 63 tombs including Tutankhamun (KV62) and Ramesses II. West Bank, Luxor.

label: Abu Simbel Temples
sub: Twin rock-cut temples of Ramesses II and Nefertari (~1264 BCE). UNESCO-relocated 1968 to save from Aswan reservoir flooding.

label: Abydos — City of Osiris
sub: Sacred city and royal burial ground from predynastic through Ramesside era. Temple of Seti I contains iconic king list.

label: Amarna (Akhetaten)
sub: Short-lived capital of Akhenaten's monotheistic revolution (~1346–1332 BCE). Abandoned after his death and deliberately erased.

label: Philae Temple (Temple of Isis)
sub: Ptolemaic-era temple complex on island in Nile, UNESCO-relocated to Agilkia Island. Last site of active ancient Egyptian religion (~400 CE).

label: Dendera Temple Complex
sub: Temple of Hathor, largely built in Ptolemaic and Roman periods. Contains famous zodiac ceiling now in the Louvre.

label: Edfu Temple (Temple of Horus)
sub: Best-preserved ancient Egyptian temple, Ptolemaic period (237–57 BCE). Dedicated to Horus, with vivid mythological reliefs.

label: Cairo — al-Qahira
sub: Founded 969 CE by Fatimid general Jawhar. Modern capital of Egypt, population ~22 million. Home of al-Azhar University and the Egyptian Museum.

label: Alexandria
sub: Founded 331 BCE by Alexander the Great. Ancient world's intellectual capital — home to the Great Library and Pharos Lighthouse. Today Egypt's second city.

label: Memphis (Mit Rahina)
sub: Ancient capital founded by Narmer (~3100 BCE). Remained Egypt's most important city for most of pharaonic history. UNESCO World Heritage site.

label: Luxor / Ancient Thebes
sub: Capital of the New Kingdom pharaohs. 'City of a Hundred Gates' (Homer). Today a city of 500,000 surrounded by ancient monuments.

label: Aswan
sub: Ancient Elephantine island trade hub; site of the Aswan High Dam (completed 1970) that transformed Egyptian agriculture and electricity.

label: Aswan High Dam
sub: Completed 1970 with Soviet aid. Controls Nile flooding, irrigates 4M feddans, generates 2.1 GW electricity. Created Lake Nasser — displaced 90,000 Nubians.

label: Suez Canal — Ismailia
sub: 193 km canal opened 1869 connecting Mediterranean to Red Sea. Nationalized 1956 by Nasser. Carries ~12% of global trade. Expanded 2015.

label: Port Said
sub: Northern entrance to Suez Canal. Scene of Anglo-French landing in Suez Crisis (1956). Key commercial port.

label: New Administrative Capital
sub: Mega-city project announced 2015, 45 km east of Cairo. $58 billion development to host all government ministries. Partially operational from 2021.

label: Tahrir Square
sub: Epicenter of the 2011 Egyptian Revolution (Jan 25–Feb 11). Millions camped here demanding Mubarak's removal. Symbol of the Arab Spring.

label: Raba'a al-Adawiya Square
sub: Site of August 14, 2013 security forces dispersal of pro-Morsi sit-in. HRW estimates 817+ killed. Symbol of Sisi crackdown on Brotherhood.

label: Sinai Peninsula
sub: Occupied by Israel 1967–1982; returned under Camp David. Active insurgency since 2013 — Sinai Province (IS affiliate) conducts attacks on Egyptian military.

label: Rafah Border Crossing
sub: Egypt–Gaza crossing. Critical humanitarian gateway during Gaza conflicts. Egypt mediates between Hamas and Israel. Crossed by hundreds of thousands of Gazans since October 2023.

label: Cairo Citadel
sub: Medieval fortress built by Saladin (1176–1183 CE). Ottoman administrative center; Muhammad Ali Mosque (1848). Site of 1811 Mamluk massacre.

label: Suez City
sub: Site of 1956 Suez Crisis Anglo-French landing and fierce 1973 October War battles. Southern entrance to Suez Canal.
